response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior/staff full-stack engineer, but the background is overwhelmingly web, mobile, and backend work in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not embedded networking security in C. Several hard requirements are unmet: no native-level (or any) Japanese, no demonstrated Linux kernel investigation or source modification, only brief C/C++ exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI), and no exposure to LAN-switch firmware, L2/L3 networking, or security protocols such as MACsec or 802.1X. Despite strong general engineering maturity, the fit for this specialized embedded security role is very low.
strengths: Senior/staff-level engineering with strong testing, TDD, and CI/CD discipline
Some low-level C/C++ experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) and embedded/hardware exposure
Broad full-stack range and proven ability to learn unfamiliar stacks quickly
concerns: No Japanese language ability (hard requirement is native-level)
No C-for-UNIX depth, Linux kernel modification, or switch/networking security experience
Career focus is web/backend, not embedded firmware or LAN-switch products

key_factors.factor: Lack of embedded C / Linux kernel / networking security experience
